new_jobs.title: Network Engineer (Kabul)
new_jobs.description:
Role Summary
The Network Engineer is responsible for the LAN/WLAN plan, documentation, configuration, maintenance, monitoring, and all hierarchical tasks that are coming through Manager-Network Infrastructures & Security.
The Network Engineer is under the direct guidance and supervision of the NIS - Manager/NIS - Supervisor to provide daily technical help to the Network Team Leader regarding the plan, implementation, and maintaining the AWCC upcoming/ongoing projects of LAN/WLAN to ensure the availability of the network/networking environment in AWCC offices and projects.

Duties and Responsibilities
Ensures effective functioning of the WLAN/LAN, Communication Support, Network Administration, and use of documenting tools focusing on the achievement of the following results:
Compliance with corporate WLAN/LAN maintenance and technology standards/policies, guidelines, and procedures for the AWCC office's information technology environment.
Identification and implementation of innovative uses of the WLAN and LAN which would enhance the AWCC project's effectiveness.
AWCC Project's plan deployment, configurations, and maintenance of the WLAN and LAN communications on ongoing/upcoming projects.
Report network operational status by gathering and prioritizing information and management projects.
Reconfiguration of new equipment and systems in WLAN and LAN and take corrective actions in case of failure.
Configure routing and switching equipment, hosted IP, and voice services.
Provide remote support to on-site engineers and end users/customers during installation.
Provide remote troubleshooting and fault find if the issues occur upon initial installation.
Liaise with project management teams, third-line engineers, and service desk engineers regularly.
Speak to customers via email and phone for initial requirement capture.
Troubleshooting and monitoring network problems: traffic, usage, and performance.
Implementation of backup and restoration procedures for all Juniper, Cisco, and other brand devices in the customer network's Core, AGG, and Access Layers.
Maintain the network access policies and controlling systems to ensure that all the corporate Access Layers and types of equipment are properly repaired and working to maintain satisfactory communications.
Maximize network performance by monitoring performance, troubleshooting network problems and outages, scheduling upgrades, and collaborating with network architects on network optimization.
Maintenance of logs of reported problems and corrective measures taken.
Administer the AWCC Projects corporate network to provide a stable and responsive environment.
Maintain up-to-date parameters of information for the network devices including network mapping, configuration backup, and so on.
Monitor the network availability 24/7 and report the stability to the Supervisor – NIS or Team Leader.
Troubleshoot WAN connection with any kind of point-to-point links to ensure stable connectivity between ISP and customer offices, remote sites, and AWCC regional offices.
The Network Engineer is a technology professional who is highly skilled in maintaining the connectivity of networks in terms of data, voice, calls, videos, and wire/wireless network service.
Experts with voice-over-internet protocol (VoIP) skills, which is a mix of voice, video, and knowledge on one network. A Network Engineer designs, tests, installs, and maintains both local VoIP systems in projects.

Job Requirements
Qualifications and Industrial Level Certification Requirements:
A Bachelor's degree in computer science/information technology or related fields is required.
Must have completed the JNCIA, JNCIS/CCNA, CCNA Wireless, and CCNP training.
Preferred certification holders (JNCIA, CCNA, CCNA wireless Network, and so on).
Work Experience Requirements:
3 to 4 years of experience in the Information Technology field.
Experience in PC support, remote communications, LAN/WLAN/WAN.
Excellent Technical skills and knowledge of Information technology Networks particularly Juniper, Cisco, Ubiquiti, and MikroTik switches, routers, and access points.
Knowledge of wireless LAN infrastructure - Wireless APs (802.11x) and experience managing wireless end-user devices.
Ability to meet deadlines, work under pressure, good negotiation and organizational skills.
Ability to provide 24/7 network services for AWCC Projects.

Submission Guidelines
The interested candidates should fill out and submit the APPLICATION FORM with complete and correct information. To have access to the APPLICATION FORM, please copy and paste the link into your Web Browser.
https://forms.gle/VpWNPaMGW3Q1ti3JA

new_jobs.title: ICT, Solutions Analyst
new_jobs.company: French Medical Institute for Mothers and Children (FMIC)
new_jobs.description:
About French Medical Institute for Mothers and Children (FMIC)
Established in 2006 through a partnership between the Government of Afghanistan, the Government of France, La Chaîne de l’Espoir, and the Aga Khan Development Network, FMIC has become Afghanistan’s leading tertiary healthcare institution and a regional centre of excellence. Operated by the Aga Khan University, FMIC delivers high-quality, patient-centred care in accordance with international standards while serving as a hub for education, research, and innovation.
Over the past two decades, FMIC has provided healthcare services to more than 2.6 million patients, performed over 6,000 cardiac surgeries, and introduced numerous medical advancements, including Afghanistan’s first open-heart surgery and a range of specialised diagnostic and treatment services. Through telemedicine, outreach programmes, and financial assistance initiatives, FMIC continues to expand access to quality healthcare for underserved populations across the country.
Beyond clinical care, FMIC plays a vital role in strengthening Afghanistan’s health system through the training of healthcare professionals, postgraduate medical education, and the promotion of innovation and evidence-based practice. As it enters its third decade of service, FMIC remains committed to excellence, equity, and sustainable healthcare development, improving health outcomes and transforming lives across Afghanistan and the region.

Job Descriptions
Regular Responsibilities
· Gather, document, and analyze business and functional requirements.
· Conduct stakeholder interviews, workshops, and process reviews.
· Translate business needs into functional and technical specifications.
· Identify opportunities for process improvement and automation.
· Design ICT solutions aligned with business objectives.
· Promote the adoption of AI-driven analytics, reporting, and decision-support tools.
· Review solution architecture and recommend enhancements.
· Participate in solution selection, evaluation, and implementation.
· Ensure compliance with organizational standards, security protocols, and policies.
· Support ICT projects throughout the project lifecycle.
· Prepare business cases, requirements documents, and process maps.
· Coordinate User Acceptance Testing (UAT).
· Monitor project deliverables and timelines.
· Develop Power Apps solutions.
· Automate processes using Power Automate.
· Create dashboards and reports using Power BI.
· Develop SharePoint Online solutions and intranet portals.
· Support Teams, Forms, and Power Pages integrations.
· Support integration between Microsoft 365 and third-party systems.
· Utilize APIs, connectors, and the Microsoft Graph API.
· Support upgrades, migrations, and system enhancements.
· Maintain solution designs and technical documentation.
· Develop user guides and training materials.
· Prepare management reports and dashboards.
· Participate in risk assessments and security reviews.
· Ensure data protection and governance requirements are met.
· Account management and access controls.
Periodic Responsibilities
· Review user access, security controls, vulnerabilities, and compliance with organizational policies
· Prepare periodic reports, maintain documentation, and provide status updates on ICT operations and projects.
· Identify opportunities for process improvement, technology enhancement, cost optimization, and future ICT initiatives.

Job Requirements
Qualification and Education:
· Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or in progress
· IT Training: Office 365 solutions
· Web Designing and Development
· Microsoft Azure Fundamentals/ Administration (Preferred.
Knowledge and competencies (State skills, abilities & traits required)
· Good communication and interpersonal skills
· Ability to work under difficult working conditions
· Data Analytics.

Submission Guidelines

To apply for this vacancy, please use the following link:
https://forms.gle/BLMeWS1VfxQc9tdY9
If the link above does not work by clicking on it, please copy and paste it in the browser address bar.
Make sure to press submit at the end of the application.
If you are not invited for a written test or interview within a four-week period of time, please consider that your application was not considered for this vacancy.
Validated copies of academic certificates/diplomas will be requested only if you are selected.

About Afghan Bureau Collaboration Office
Afghan Bureau Collaboration Office (ABCO) is a national Afghan NGO established in 1993. ABCO is operating in all regions of Afghanistan with a mission of national Food Security and Livelihood Resilience Building. For the last 27 years ABCO has been committed to its mission and has performed plenty of programs and projects with UN and international organizations. ABCO has a commitment of transparency and accountability with its partners and clients. Following a commitment of self-improvement, integrity and long-term engagement and support with its personnel and project staff.

Job Descriptions
Responsibilities:
· Supervise electrical installation, power distribution, lighting, and low current systems.
· Install, test, and commission ICT and structured cabling systems.
· Coordinate with mechanical and civil teams for integration of conduits and wiring.
· Ensure compliance with electrical safety standards and best practices.
· Maintain logs of installations, inspections, and testing results.
Responsibilities:
· Direct daily activities of skilled and unskilled workers.
· Monitor adherence to quality, safety, and schedule requirements.
· Report progress and issues to engineers and project manager.
· Ensure proper use of tools, PPE, and compliance with site safety protocols.
· Coordinate workforce assignments and resolve on-site technical problems.

Job Requirements
Electrical Engineer / IT & Light Current Engineer
Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Electrical or IT Engineering; 10–15 years of experience.

Interested qualified candidates should submit their CVs with a cover letter explaining their motivation in applying for the job and highlighting their relevant skills and experience to the following email address:
hr@afghanbureau.org .
Candidates must clearly write the Reference and Job Title in the subject of line otherwise your application will not be considered.
We have equal opportunities for male, female and those with disabilities, so we strongly encourage qualified female and those with disability candidates to apply.
We are committed to ethical standards, a safe and inclusive work environment. Our work is based on our values: Responsiveness, Equality, Impartiality, Social Justice, and Integrity.
ABCO has a zero-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of ABCO, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority, and discrimination. ABCO also adheres to strict child safeguarding principles. Therefore, all candidates will have background and reference checks, which are part of the recruitment process and will be conducted for the successful candidate.

expiring_today.title: Internal Audit Officer (IT)
expiring_today.description:
Role Summary
The incumbent of this position will help the organization by protecting its internal controls and data within its technology system. He/She will safeguard sensitive information by identifying weaknesses in the system’s network and creating strategies to prevent any security breaches in the technology.

Duties and Responsibilities
Assist the Internal Audit team in developing the drafted annual Internal Audit plan.
Support the Internal Audit team in developing and implementing the evolving Internal Audit methodology for AWCC.
Review external IT Audit and inspection reports and ensure that all recommendations in them have been followed.
Create a solid information technology infrastructure and collaborate with clients to devise and put in place policies and procedures regarding network security issues.
Perform information system control reviews including system development standards, operating procedures, system security, communication controls, backup and disaster recovery, and system maintenance.
Assist in IT audit, IT risk management, and IT compliance activities.
Conduct all assigned audits including planning, evaluating, and documenting the results, report, and follow-up in accordance with the audit plan.
Assume responsibility as team leader for special audit assignments and submit evaluation results on assigned audits to the Internal Audit Manager.
Identify the weaknesses in systems, network, or database levels of information systems and provide recommendations to prevent security breaches in the information system based on technical assessments.
Propose practical and value-added recommendations to address/control weaknesses and/or process inefficiencies.
Check and monitor all servers, applications, printers, scanners, copiers, computer systems, user privileges, helpdesk services, and all related systems relevant to the System Admin section. (System Administration)
Monitor the recording of all cameras and sound systems, hard drives, DVR’s, sound systems, and all systems related to the CCTV section.
Develop & maintain effective and professional working relationships with all levels of staff within the company.
Assist the Internal Audit Manager in the preparation of a concise and informative audit report to effectively communicate the findings and recommendations to the Department and senior management.
Organize & reference work papers for review by Audit Manager.
Follow up on audit findings to ensure that the related section has taken corrective action against noted internal Audit department findings.
Other responsibilities assigned by the Internal Audit Manager.

Job Requirements
Bachelor's degree in computer science, a Master's degree is preferable. (CCNA certificate is an asset)
Good knowledge of telecommunication laws, rules, and regulations.
At least 2-3 years of related experience in the Audit & IT Department.
Fluent communication skills (written and verbal) in local languages as well as English.
Professional training or certificates in IT & Audit-related fields are a plus.
Understanding of multiple technology domains, including software development, Windows, Database Management, and Networking.
Capable of working independently and with minimal supervision.
Able to travel to all provinces of Afghanistan.
Outstanding interpersonal and communication skills; ability to communicate effectively with technical and non-technical audiences.
Expert knowledge of internal auditing, internal controls, and risk management.
Comprehensive understanding of internal control environments within the IT function.
Excellent leadership and teamwork skills.
Proactive, hands-on, results-driven orientation required.

Submission Guidelines
Interested candidates should fill out and submit the APPLICATION FORM with complete and correct information. To have access to the APPLICATION FORM, please copy and paste the link into your Web Browser.

Additionally, the IT Technician provides technical support regarding computer operating systems, software applications, email configuration, installation processes, printing solutions, and the maintenance of customer LAN networks.

Perform installation, activation, and troubleshooting of wireless links in accordance with company guidelines.
Execute the installation, activation, and troubleshooting of microwave links following established protocols.
Conduct installation, activation, and troubleshooting of VSAT links while adhering to company policies.
Install and configure LAN network devices to meet customer requirements effectively.
Troubleshoot LAN network devices and provide the best solution in case of second option availability.
Conduct surveys for network projects, arrange requirements, and implement solutions as needed.
DVR camera wiring, installation, and configuring based on the requirement.
UAT wireless base stations maintenance and resolving 1st level base station issues (in cooperation with Head Office)
Installing Wire and Wireless equipment at UA wireless base stations and ensuring 100% functionality upon completion.
Integrating technical solutions successfully done on-site and sharing it with other team members for future use.
Detailed daily and weekly reports to under-assigned manager.

Job Requirements
Bachelor’s degree/Diploma in Computer Science and/ or relevant field.
A minimum of 3 years of experience in network installation and troubleshooting or a relevant field.
Relevant certifications (e.g., Cisco CCNA, MCITP, or equivalent) will be considered an advantage.
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to communicate technical information effectively.
Full understanding and speaking of Dari, Pashto, and English.
Ability to ride a motorcycle and be comfortable using it when required for work-related tasks

Dependability — The job requires being reliable, responsible, dependable, and fulfilling obligations.
Attention to Detail — The job requires being careful about detail and thorough in completing work tasks.

Integrity — The job requires being honest and ethical.

expiring_today.title: System Administrator
expiring_today.description:
Role Summary
The System Administrator is responsible for the end-to-end management and security of the ISP’s core network infrastructures, including firewalls, routers, switches, and servers across multiple sites. This role supports customers by ensuring stable internet connectivity and reliable service through precise configuration, continuous monitoring, and rapid incident resolution. It also ensures maximum network uptime and peak performance across the infrastructure. Key responsibilities include managing link provisioning, bandwidth allocation, IP address management, and collaboration with upstream providers.

Duties and Responsibilities
Network Infrastructure & Security
· Install, configure, and maintain core routers, switches, and firewalls, ensuring scalability and adaptability to future growth.
· Oversee the operation and firmware updates of primary and secondary firewalls across Main Office and Provincial sites, ensuring optimal performance and security.
· Monitor spanning tree protocols on switches, ensuring data flows through primary point-to-point links and addressing any issues.
· Protect CPE and PTP devices from external network attacks by blocking unauthorized ports and ensuring secure network access.
· Identify customer bandwidth over-usage through weekly monitoring via Cacti servers, apply traffic shaping on CPE devices if limiters fail, and ensure no customer exceeds their contracted bandwidth.
2. Link & Bandwidth Management
· Process link activations, upgrades, downgrades, and suspensions through CRM and coordination with the collections department, ensuring seamless integration with core firewall systems.
· Administer the firewall system dedicated to hotspot users, generating new accounts and allocating bandwidth based on user requirements.
· Record customers’ day and night bandwidth usage and update the records daily; monitor to prevent overload during peak hours, and request bandwidth upgrades for the main office and branches as needed.
· Manage firewall time schedulers for day and night bandwidth allocations, optimizing network usage.
· Oversee demo internet link connections, ensuring customer satisfaction during trial periods.
· Administer customer requests for temporary bandwidth upgrades, ensuring seamless service delivery.
3. Server & System Maintenance
· Conduct monthly HDD diagnostics on the primary server, ensuring data security and system health.
· Maintain and update the Primary and Secondary Cacti servers to ensure better functionality and apply available updates.
· Monitor UCRM server health and apply updates as they become available.
· Monitor NTP server health, specifically for PTP, CPE, and the bandwidth scheduler.
· Maintain EMCO server IP addresses and register new PTP and PTMP links for the alerting system in case of downtime.
4. IP & Device Management
· Update IP allocation sheets for Main office and provincial branches, including devices license keys.
· Monitor the locations of high-capacity devices and their connected base stations.
· Maintain records of customers and sites’ equipment serial numbers for asset record.
5. Monitoring & Troubleshooting
· Identify and resolve WAN, LAN, and WLAN issues promptly to maintain network stability.
· Utilize Intermapper to assess microwave device capacities, ensuring no device is overloaded.
· Update and monitor the WLAN virtualized Layer 2 map for new sector and switch installations, ensuring all PTP devices operate above 10 Mbps LAN speed by actively monitoring the alerting system.
· Oversee the number of tickets created and closed by System/Helpdesk officers, ensuring timely and satisfactory resolution of technical issues.
6. Customer Support & Collaboration
· Collaborate quarterly with the collections department to reconcile active customer bandwidth allocations, including day and night usage.
· Provide online support to team members and customers for any technical-related issues, ensuring prompt resolution.
· Upstream Coordination: Serve as the primary liaison with upstream providers to diagnose and resolve link issues promptly, staying engaged until full resolution to ensure uninterrupted service.
7. Compliance & Web Filtering
· Continuously monitor web filter subnets and liaise with upstream providers to block URLs flagged by ATRA, ensuring permanent enforcement in full compliance with its official directives.
8. Additional Duties
· Perform any other tasks as assigned by management to support network operations and service delivery.

Job Requirements
Required: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Network Engineering, or a related field. Preference will be given to candidates with a Master’s degree in a related major field.
With a Bachelor’s degree: minimum of 5 years’ experience in system or network administration; with a Master’s degree: minimum of 3 years, preferably within an ISP environment.
CCNA and CCNP certifications are highly preferred.
Strong hands-on experience with Cisco, MikroTik, and Ubiquiti (UBQT) devices.
Advanced knowledge of network protocols, including TCP/IP, BGP, OSPF, VLANs, and PPPoE.
Deep understanding of network security best practices and compliance with regulatory directives (e.g., ATRA).
Ability to analyze and resolve complex technical issues efficiently under pressure.
Excellent communication and reporting skills in Dari, Pashto, and English.

Submission Guidelines
Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications by Jun 10, 2026 . To apply, please send your updated CV to vacancies@uatelecom.af
In the subject line of your email, please ensure to include the Vacancy Number and Desired Province. e.g: (UAT-VA-SA-2152-J - Kabul) .
Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for the written test or interview process.

About Action Against Hunger-ACF
Action Against Hunger (ACF) is a Non-Governmental Organization (NGO) that was created in France in1979. ACF has been active in Afghanistan in the medical, nutrition, food security, and water and sanitation fields since 1995.ACF improved access to health and nutrition services for the most vulnerable people, who live in remote areas without a localhealth system. In particular, the country team supported pregnant and breastfeeding women and children under five, who are atgreater risk of malnutrition. Nutrition programs rely on an integrated approach, addressing both primary and underlying causes, inorder to have a lasting impact on nutritional status. The country team worked at community level, in villages and at provincial level,strengthening the public health system. It also worked at national level to ensure reliable information about the nutritional situation reached the wider humanitarian community. Covid-19 prevention was integrated into all interventions and additional responseactivities were implemented, such as the disinfection of health facilities and public places, cash assistance, psychosocial support andthe distribution of hygiene kits to affected populations.

expiring_soon.salary: As per the organization's salary scale
expiring_soon.description:
About ACDO
Asia Community Development Organization (ACDO) is an Afghan non-governmental, non-political, and non-profit humanitarian organization established in 2014 and registered with the Government of Afghanistan under the supervision of the Ministry of Economy (MoEc) with a mission to provide humanitarian and development assistance to the vulnerable, at-risk, and underserved people throughout the country.
ACDO responds to the needs of deprived and marginalized rural and urban communities by managing and utilizing the available scarce resources in a well-organized, efficient, effective, transparent, and dignified manner to enhance resilience, boost access to education, health services, and infrastructure, ensure food and livelihood security, and promote social protection and inclusion.
ACDO has an active presence in most parts of the country and intends to expand its operations to the remaining provinces, as to transform itself into a leading and developed organization that will deliver to and support large number of people who have been affected by the long-protracted and sudden on-set emergencies and regardless of the hardest circumstances that exist in Afghanistan, ACDO has managed to deliver humanitarian and development assistance in most of country.

Job Descriptions
The Community-based Climate-Responsive Livelihoods, Forestry, and Rangeland Management Project aims to strengthen the resilience of vulnerable rural communities through sustainable natural resource management, climate-resilient livelihoods, environmental restoration, and community-driven development interventions in Kuner Province.
The project will support local communities in improving livelihoods through skills development, sustainable forestry and rangeland management practices, rehabilitation of community assets, promotion of climate adaptation measures, and enhanced community participation in natural resource governance. The intervention seeks to improve household income opportunities, strengthen environmental sustainability, and increase community resilience to climate-related risks and challenges.
To support the implementation of this initiative, a roster of qualified professionals will be established for potential engagement in technical, managerial, administrative, training, monitoring, engineering, and community outreach roles. Successful candidates will be considered for assignment based on project needs, funding approval, operational requirements, and final project staffing plans.
Interested candidates are invited to submit their CVs and expressions of interest for inclusion in the project roster. Shortlisted candidates may be contacted for further assessment and verification should the project proceed to implementation .

Job Requirements
Minimum 5 years of relevant experience in GIS mapping and environmental assessment.
Minimum Bachelor’s degree in GIS, Environmental Science or related discipline.
Experience in spatial analysis, environmental screening and site selection
Familiarity with local socio-political context and fragile environments in Afghanistan.
Experience in implementing UNDP/UN or donor-funded projects.
Experience in leading multidisciplinary teams and ensuring gender inclusion.
Skills and Competencies:
Strong coordination, reporting, and management skills.
Proficiency in oral and written English is required.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Good report writing and data verification skills.
Ability to work in remote and challenging environments.
Problem-solving and decision-making abilities.
Proficiency in local languages (Pashto/Dari) and basic English skills.
Key Deliverables:
Regular district monitoring reports
Verified and accurate field data
Effective coordination with stakeholders
Improved quality of program implementation
Timely identification and resolution of field issues
Key Performance Indicators (KPIs):
Achievement of district-level targets
Quality and timeliness of reports
Staff performance and supervision effectiveness
Community engagement and satisfaction
Compliance with donor and organizational standards
Working Conditions:
Based in the district with frequent travel to project sites (schools/communities)
May require working under difficult field conditions
Close coordination with provincial and central teams

Submission Guidelines
Qualified applicants are highly encouraged to apply for the position and submit their applications (Cover Letter and CV together), NOT Educational and Work Certificates, to the attention of the Human Resource Department at https://acdo.org/apply/gis-and-environmental-specialist-roster-161 until 04:00 pm (Afghanistan Standard Time) June 13, 2026.
Shortlisted candidates will be directly contacted for a written test and, consequently, for an interview. If you are not contacted TWO WEEKS after the closing date, please know that your application has not been successful for the post.

Submission Email
N/A
Apply

About AKHS-A (Aga Khan Health Services-Afghanistan)
Aga Khan Health Services (AKHS) is one of the most comprehensive private, not‐for‐profit health care systems in the developing world with community health programs in large geographical areas in Central and South Asia, as well as in East Africa.
Aga Khan Health Services (AKHS) is one of the most comprehensive private, not‐for‐profit health care systems in the developing world with community health programs in large geographical areas in Central and South Asia, as well as in East Africa.
The Aga Khan Health Services, Afghanistan (AKHS, A) was established in 2003 as part of the Aga Khan Development Network (AKDN). AKHS, A provides health care services mainly on behalf of the government including the Ministry of Public Health’s of Afghanistan’s Basic Package of Health Services (BPHS) and Essential Package of Hospital Services (EPHS). AKHS, A is unique both in scale and scope, delivering critical health services along the continuum of care, from Community Health Workers (CHWs) to ISO certified hospital facilities.

AKHS, A provides health care services mainly on behalf of the government including the Ministry of Public Health’s of Afghanistan’s Basic Package of Health Services (BPHS) and Essential Package of Hospital Services (EPHS). AKHS, A is unique both in scale and scope, delivering critical health services along the continuum of care, from Community Health Workers (CHWs) to ISO certified hospital facilities.
AKHS, A is currently implementing healthcare services in Bamyan, Baghlan, Badakhshan, Kabul and Takhar provinces. The agency’s scope of work includes the provision of primary and secondary healthcare, preventive and curative medical care, Nursing and Midwifery education, Human Resources Development and Hospital Management. As BPHS and EPHS implementer, AKHS, A provides technical support as well as capacity building of health personnel, immunization services, nutrition trainings and the training of Community Health Nurses and Community Health Midwives.

expiring_soon.description:
Role Summary
پوهنتون کاتب استاد کادر دیپارتمنت انجنیری نرم افزار استخدام می‌کند.

Duties and Responsibilities
الف- رعایت نمودن مقررات و طرزالعمل های پوهنتون
ب- تعهد به حضور و وقت شناسی
ج- ارائه گزارش ماهوار، سمستروار و سالانه به رئیس پوهنحی با فرمت تعیین شده از سوی ریاست پوهنحی
د- تهیه تمامی مواد درسی مورد نیاز مضامین مربوط جهت تدریس
ه- تهیه رهنمود دروس عملی و کار ساحوی (در صورت موجودیت)
و- تهیه پلان عملیاتی انفرادی مطابق معیارهای آکادمیک
ز- تهیه کورس پالیسی و ارائه آن در ابتدای هر سمستر به محصلین و پوهنحی
ح- پیشبرد منظم دروس نظری و عملی مطابق تقسیم اوقات ارائه شده از سوی پوهنحی
ط- انجام حضور و غیاب محصلین و ثبت آن در کتاب حاضری در هر جلسه درسی
ی- ارزیابی محصلین مربوط، در طول سمستر و ختم آن مطابق به مقررات پوهنتون
ک- راهنمایی محصلین در امور آموزشی به عنوان استاد راهنما بر اساس هدایت پوهنحی
ل- راهنمایی محصلین در تهیه مونوگراف به عنوان استاد راهنما و یا مشاور
م- ارزیابی آثار و کنفرانس های علمی استادان و داوری مونوگراف و مقالات محصلین
ن- اشتراک فعال و سهم گیری در محافل علمی پوهنحی و پوهنتون
س- اشتراک در برنامه های آموزشی جهت ارتقای ظرفیت استادان
ع- التزام به حضور در کمیته ها، مجالس و شوراهای مربوط، در پوهنحی و پوهنتون و انجام مسئولیت های محوله
ف- سهم گیری مسئولانه در بهبود امور پوهنحی و پوهنتون به منظور توسعه علمی، آموزشی
ص- تلاش برای ارتقای سویه علمی خویش و کسب مهارت های کاری در ارتباط با وظیفه
ق- ترجمه و یا تألیف کتب درسی، کتب ممد درسی، مقالات و کمک به پیشبرد تحقیقات علمی پوهنتون
ر- اشتراک فعال و منظم در برگزاری امتحانات پوهنتون و انجام مسئولیت های سپرده شده
ش- حفظ اسرار و جلوگیری از انتقال داشته های مادی و معنوی پوهنتون به افراد و نهادهای دیگر بدون کسب اجازه از مقامات ذیربط
ت- سهم گیری در امور جامعه و کمک به انکشاف آن
ث- انجام سایر وظایف آکادمیک که از سوی آمر دیپارتمنت و یا رئیس پوهنحی محول می گردد

Job Requirements
الف- ماستري او دوکتورا(هوش مصنوعی، دیتاساینس، انجنیری نرم افزار)
ب- تسلط به زبانهای ملی و زبان انگلیسی

متقاضیان محترم می توانند به صورت حضوری به مدیریت استادان و تدریسی پوهنتون کاتب مراجعه نمایند:

tech_banking_jobs.title: Sharia Audit Officer
tech_banking_jobs.description:
Role Summary
The Sharia Audit Officer is responsible for conducting periodic Sharia audits across the bank to ensure all products, services, contracts, transactions, and operational processes fully comply with Islamic Sharia principles and the rulings of the Sharia Supervisory Board. The role helps preserve the bank’s Islamic integrity and strengthens governance.

Duties and Responsibilities
1. Sharia Audit Planning & Execution
Participate in preparing the annual Sharia Audit Plan based on risk assessment.
Conduct field audits in branches and departments to verify adherence to Sharia guidelines.
Examine financing files, contracts, documentation, and transaction flows for Sharia compliance.
Test samples of Murabaha, Ijara, Musharaka, Mudaraba, and deposit transactions against Sharia standards.
2. Review of Contracts, Policies & Procedures
Audit the implementation of Sharia-approved product structures and documentation.
Ensure operational procedures match the product manuals approved by the Sharia Supervisory Board (SSB).
Check whether staff are following required Sharia workflows (e.g., asset ownership, agency structures, disclosures, penalties handling).
3. Identification of Sharia Non-Compliance
Identify Sharia violations, deviations, or gaps during audits.
Discuss findings with relevant departments and agree on corrective actions.
Ensure non-compliance events are recorded, reported, and resolved within the required timeframe.
4. Reporting & Documentation
Prepare clear Sharia audit reports with observations, recommendations, and compliance ratings.
Submit audit results to the Head of Sharia Audit and contribute to reports for the Sharia Supervisory Board.
Maintain proper documentation of all audit working papers and evidence.
5. Follow-Up & Corrective Actions
Follow up on previously reported Sharia findings to verify whether corrective actions were implemented.
Work with business units, risk management, operations, and compliance teams to close audit issues.
6. Coordination & Support
Coordinate with Internal Audit and Compliance Department for combined risk reviews.
Provide Sharia guidance to departments during audits to improve their understanding and performance.
7. Capacity Building & Awareness
Support the development of Sharia audit tools, checklists, and audit methodologies.
Assist in internal awareness sessions on Sharia compliance requirements for staff.
8. Continuous Improvement
Stay updated on AAOIFI Sharia standards, new fatwas, and industry best practices.
. Recommend improvements to Sharia audit processes and internal controls

Job Requirements
Qualifications & Experience
Bachelor’s degree in Islamic Studies, Sharia, Islamic Finance, Accounting, or Business Administration.
Professional certification in Islamic finance or Sharia audit (preferred).
1–3 years of experience in Islamic banking, Sharia audit, internal audit, or compliance roles.
Familiarity with AAOIFI standards and Islamic banking products.
Skills & Competencies
Strong understanding of Islamic banking and Sharia principles.
Ability to analyze financial transactions and documentation.
Good report writing skills with clarity and accuracy.
Strong attention to detail and analytical mindset.
Excellent communication and coordination abilities.
High integrity, objectivity, and professionalism.

Submission Guidelines
Applicants who meet the above requirements should submit their updated Curriculum vitae (CV)/Resume to (sr.recruitment-officer@ghazanfarbank.com)
Please Mention specific Job Title in subject of your E-mail otherwise your Email will not be considered.
Note: only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further recruitment process.

tech_banking_jobs.title: Digital Banking Operation Manager (Re-announced)
tech_banking_jobs.description:
Role Summary
The Digital Banking Operations Manager is responsible for overseeing the daily operational activities and ensuring the smooth functioning of key units within the Digital Banking Department. This includes Card Issuance and Management, Settlement, Refund & Dispute Resolution, Customer Service and Support, and Business & Operations. The role ensures efficient service delivery, operational excellence, compliance with regulatory requirements, and enhanced customer experience across all digital banking services.

Duties and Responsibilities
Card Issuance and Management:
Supervise the end-to-end card issuance process including debit, credit, prepaid and instant AfPay, MasterCards & VISA.
Coordinate with vendors and internal stakeholders for timely card production, delivery, and stock management.
Ensure compliance with card security standards and central bank regulations.
Settlement, Refund & Dispute Resolution:
Oversee daily transaction settlement and reconciliation processes with networks such as MasterCard, Visa, and AfPay.
Manage refund processing and timely resolution of customer disputes and chargebacks.
Coordinate with Finance, IT, and external partners to ensure settlement accuracy and integrity.
Customer Service and Support:
Lead the digital banking support team to provide timely and effective responses to customer inquiries and issues.
Monitor service levels and continuously improve customer support processes.
Handle escalated cases and ensure a professional and solution-oriented response.
Coordinate with the marketing to effectively and efficiently advertise the digital banking products and services to attract customers and share accurate information about the AUB digital banking products and services in social medias and website.
Leads the call center team for timely support for the digital banking services and products.
Business and Operations:
Coordinate daily business operations of the Digital Banking Department.
Implement and monitor SOPs for all operational activities.
Liaise with internal departments (IT, Risk, Compliance, Finance, etc.) for issue resolution and service enhancements.
Track KPIs and generate performance reports for senior management.
Lead accruals booking and manage digital banking operation cost and expenses.
Cross-functional Responsibilities:
Work closely with the Digital Banking Technical Manager and Deputy Head of DBD to align operations with technical developments and strategic initiatives.
Support the rollout of new digital products and services.
Ensure readiness for audits, regulatory reviews, and compliance reporting.
Reporting and Documentation:
Maintain detailed technical documentation, architecture designs, and process flows for all managed systems.
Provide regular performance, risk, and incident reports to the Deputy Head and Head of DBD.

Job Requirements
Education:
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Business Administration, Finance or related field (Master’s preferred).
Experience:
Minimum of 5 years of experience in digital banking systems, with at least 2 years in a technical leadership role.
Technical Competencies:
Strong understanding of digital banking services, card operations, and payment systems.
Experience in settlement and dispute management.
Excellent problem-solving, leadership, and communication skills.
Familiarity with core banking systems and digital platforms.
Fluency in English and local languages (Dari/Pashto) is an advantage.
Knowledge of Core Banking Systems (CBS).
Other Skills:
Strong leadership and problem-solving abilities.
Excellent communication and coordination skills.
Fluency in English; knowledge of Dari and Pashto is an advantage.

Submission Guidelines
Interested candidates who deem themselves suitable for this vacancy can share their complete profile including (application letter, updated CV, and recent photo) electronically to below mentioned email and please indicate the vacancy number AUB/EA/VA/026-018 in the subject line of your email otherwise your application will not be considered.

Role Summary
Conducting field research in Baghlan Province, Afghanistan, or writing research reports on Afghanistan-related topics.
Requires quick work execution, competent English skills, honesty and reliability, and extensive knowledge or connections in Baghlan Province.

Duties and Responsibilities
Conducting field research in Baghlan Province, Afghanistan, or writing research reports on Afghanistan-related topics.

Job Requirements
Residing in Baghlan Province, Afghanistan; possessing basic English writing and communication skills; preference will be given to candidates with relevant government, military, or investigator experience; male candidates preferred

Submission Guidelines
Please submit your English resume on this website. Name your resume with your name and occupation. The resume should include your phone number, education, and detailed work experience (specifying position, company name, and responsibilities). Please update your resume regularly. Company staff will contact you via email; please check your inbox.
